summary:
Lag screw fixation stabilizes the fracture for better healing by distributing loads evenly, thus enhancing mobility post-injury; multiple studies confirm its effectiveness through improved force management around the injury area. ``` Summary:
Lag screw fixation helps maintain stable bone alignment during healing by effectively managing distributed loads, leading to reduced complications and facilitating early rehabilitation. Studies consistently show it supports optimal structural integrity and preserves joint functionality compared to other methods.
```

The summary captures the essential aspects—load-sharing capability aiding stability and faster recovery—in just two concise statements as requested.
